expectations.text: Identifies that 'meal planning' and 'meal prep' are missing from the current description
expectations.evidence: Workflow C under-triggering: 'Ask for 3 example queries that should have triggered but didn't. Compare against the description field word by word. Identify missing keywords or user-intent phrases.' Applied to 'meal planning' / 'weekly meal prep' vs 'Helps users find and adapt recipes for dietary restrictions' — gap is immediately identifiable.

expectations.text: Suggests specific phrases to add to the description
expectations.evidence: Workflow C: 'Suggest concrete additions — and produce a revised description draft.' Not just advice — a draft is required.

expectations.text: Explains the mechanism: Claude matches descriptions to queries by keyword/concept coverage
expectations.evidence: Workflow C explains: 'descriptions should focus on the user's intent, not the skill's implementation.' Notes page expands: 'A great skill that never triggers is invisible.' The principle is present even if not phrased as a technical mechanism.

expectations.text: Produces a revised description draft (not just advice)
expectations.evidence: Workflow C explicitly says 'produce a revised description draft' — not 'suggest improvements'. This is an action step, not guidance.
